# F019. Correlation Graph + LightGBM Overlay

## Positioning

This candidate uses correlation structure as an extra context layer on top of the tabular score model. The graph itself is a research extension, but the operational path remains the same ranker-plus-risk stack.

## Reference Basis

- [Graph Neural Networks for Financial Forecasting](../../references/papers/5_08_Graph_Neural_Networks_for_Financial_Forecasting.pdf) - graph-based financial forecasting reference
- [Portfolio Management with Graph Neural Networks](../../references/papers/2_09_Portfolio_Management_with_Graph_Neural_Networks.pdf) - graph-conditioned allocation and diversification
- [LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree](../../references/papers/lightgbm_neurips_2017.pdf) - score model sitting on top of the graph features

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Graph and Correlation Stack

Correlation graphs and sector relations for diversification-aware ranking.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - cross-asset data loader
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - graph and correlation feature construction
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - graph-aware score model

### Cross-Sectional Ranker

The executable ranking path that turns scores into a tradable long/flat or top-k book.
- [src/models/lgbm.py](../../../src/models/lgbm.py) - LightGBM scoring model
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - feature matrix construction for the ranker

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Build correlation or sector graphs from point-in-time cross-asset returns.
- Keep graph edges stable enough that the score model is not learning random window noise.
- Join graph context with the standard technical and lagged feature set in the builder.

## Execution Path

- Use graph-aware features to adjust the ranker score or the portfolio concentration limit.
- Let the graph overlay act as a diversification haircut rather than as a direct trade trigger.
- Run the resulting signal through the normal backtest and paper-safe rails.

## Risk Controls

- Keep the graph window stable and point-in-time; unstable edges are worse than no graph at all.
- Do not let graph features silently overfit one short window.
- Respect the same cost and exposure rules that govern F001.

## Validation Gates

- Compare concentration, turnover, and drawdown against F001 and F002.
- Check whether the graph overlay improves diversification more than it adds instability.
- Verify that the graph features survive a holdout regime rather than only the training window.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the graph windows are too noisy to maintain.
- Archive if diversification improves only on paper and not after costs.
- Archive if the overlay cannot be kept point-in-time.

## Next Step

Use this as the graph-aware diversification test for future factor work.
